memoryArtifacts.headline: History of Edmund Tapp, Sr.  (ca.1550 - 1635)
memoryArtifacts.description:
by Garry Bryant

Edmund Tapp, Sr.
College graduate
(c.1550 - 1635)

Born about 1550. Edmund attended Caius College in Cambridgshire in 1568 when he was 18. Married about 1585 to Margaret ???. The wido …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Edmund Tapp Sr.於1550年出生在英國英格蘭貝德福爾費爾莫沙安時，他的父親Robert Tapp36歲，母親Margaret Barley25歲。他和Margaret至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1635年5月2日在英國賀特福爾貝寧頓享年85歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀特福爾貝寧頓。

nameMeanings.name: Tapp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the early Middle English personal name Tapp (Old English Tæppa). The unrecorded Old English personal name is thought to be the first element of the placenames Taplow (Buckinghamshire), Tapners (Kent), Tappington (Kent), and Tapton (Derbyshire). See also Taplin .
English: possibly a nickname from Middle English tape ‘ribbon, tape’, or tappe ‘plug, tap’; ‘tap, knock’.

variants.name: Tappe
meanings: North German: metonymic occupational name for the owner of a tavern, a beer or wine tapper, from Middle Low German tappe ‘tap’.
English: variant of   Tapp  . This surname has now died out in Britain.

variants.name: Taplin
meanings: probably from Middle English Tolpayn, which may be a nickname for a miller, from Middle English tollen ‘to exact a charge on (something)’ + Middle English (Old French) pain ‘bread’, though the tolls that the miller exacted were on the flour he ground not the bread that was made from it. Alternatively, if -payne is a spelling of Middle English (Old French) peine ‘tax’, it may instead be a nickname for a tax collector.

variants.name: Tapscott
meanings: English (Devon): habitational name from a lost Devon placename, perhaps in or near Barnstaple, recorded as Tappyscote in 1504–15. The placename may derive from an early Middle English personal name Tapp (Old English Tæppa), genitive Tappes, + Middle English cot ‘cottage, hut, shelter’, but this cannot be certain due to a lack of medieval forms. See also Tapp for the Middle English personal name Tapp, which is most frequent in Devon.

nameMeanings.name: Edmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + mund ‘protector’. It was borne by several early royal and saintly figures, including a 9th-century king of East Anglia killed by invading Danes, allegedly for his adherence to Christianity. In the 16th and 17th centuries, there was a good deal of interchange between Edmund and Edward.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Edmund Tapp Sr. was born in 1550, in Felmersham, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert Tapp, was 36 and his mother, Margaret Barley, was 25. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Margaret. He died on 2 May 1635, in Benington, Hertfordshire, England, at the age of 85, and was buried in Benington, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edmund Tapp Sr. est né en 1550, à Felmersham, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert Tapp, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Margaret Barley, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Margaret. Il est décédé le 2 mai 1635, à Benington, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 85 ans, et a été enterré à Benington, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Edmund Tapp Sr.が1550年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアフエルメルシャムで生まれた時，父Robert Tappは36歳，母Margaret Barleyは25歳であった。彼はMargaretとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1635年5月2日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアベニントンで85歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアベニントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Edmund Tapp Sr.(이)가 1550년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 프머샤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Tapp(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Barley(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Margaret(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1635년 5월 2일 영국 허트퍼드실르 베닝턴에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 베닝턴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edmund Tapp Sr. родился в 1550 г. в местности фелмиршам, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Robert Tapp было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Barley, 25. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Margaret. Он умер 2 мая 1635 г. в местности бенингтон, хиртфордшир, Англия в возрасте 85 лет и был похоронен в местности бенингтон, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
